channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Prosecutor Fani Willis is removed from the Georgia election case against Trump and others
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A state appeals court on Thursday removed Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis from the Georgia election interference case against Donald Trump and others, the latest legal victory for the president-elect in criminal cases that once threatened his career and freedom.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump backs plan B spending deal, urges swift passage
item.description: President-elect Trump on Thursday backed a new spending deal brokered by House Republicans to avert a shutdown after an initial proposal was torpedoed amid opposition from Trump and several of his allies.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon warns that a government shutdown will cost troops their pay over the holidays
item.description: The Pentagon is warning that Republicans’ decision to abandon a congressional spending plan will cost troops their paychecks over the holidays unless some agreement is reached before Friday’s deadline to prevent a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor And Tara Copp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Republicans announce funding agreement, Thursday vote as shutdown looms
item.description: House Republicans have reached an agreement on a plan B to avert a government shutdown ahead of Friday’s deadline and plan to vote on Thursday, lawmakers announced.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: ICE nabs several migrants convicted of child molestation, one convicted murderer, in blue state suburbs
item.description: Baltimore ICE Enforcement and Removal Operations officials arrested eight criminal illegal immigrants in suburban Maryland, including four convicted of child molestation and one murderer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about the debt ceiling debate as a government shutdown looms in Washington
item.description: A debate over the debt ceiling is at the center of a dispute over funding that is pushing the possibility of a federal government shutdown to the brink in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: House GOP eyes plan B stopgap with separate 2-part debt limit commitment
item.description: House Republicans are eyeing an alternative government funding stopgap that excludes a debt ceiling increase, and are instead working on a commitment to raise the borrowing limit twice next year in a bid to appease President-elect Trump, two sources told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: DC celebrates the start of $800M arena renovation as potential football stadium progress falters
item.description: Washington Capitals and Wizards owner Ted Leonsis, NBA Commissioner Adam Silver, District of Columbia Mayor Muriel Bowser and other area leaders celebrated the start of an $800 million downtown arena renovation Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Whyno, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jeffries says bipartisan funding deal is ‘best path,’ but doesn’t rule out thinner bill
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) said Thursday the spending deal secured by the two parties is the “best path” for avoiding a government shutdown, but he did not rule out Democratic support for a smaller package to keep the lights on into next year.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's what happens during a partial government shutdown
item.description: During a partial government shutdown, nonessential services pause and federal workers face furloughs or delayed pay, but essential operations will continue.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Veteran Fox News business anchor Neil Cavuto leaving after 28 years
item.description: Neil Cavuto, a business journalist who hosts a weekday afternoon show on Fox News Channel and has been with the network since its inception in 1996, is leaving after Thursday's show, Fox said.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rand Paul suggests replacing Mike Johnson with Elon Musk as Speaker of the House
item.description: Sen. Rand Paul, R-Ky., has floated the idea of replacing House Speaker Mike Johnson, R-La., with Elon Musk after the chaotic collapse of the continuing resolution spending bill.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump backs new GOP plan to fund government and raise debt limit as shutdown nears
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has announced “success” in coming up with a new bill to fund the government and lift the debt ceiling a day before a shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro And Kevin Freking, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Johnson spending deal throws Speakership into question as floor vote approaches
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson’s handling of an end-of-year spending deal is throwing his grasp on the gavel into uncertain territory ahead of a critical Jan. 3 Speaker vote, as some GOP lawmakers question their support for the Louisiana Republican. Rep. Thomas Massie (R-Ky.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers caught off guard by Trump debt ceiling demand
item.description: Lawmakers on both sides of the aisle appeared caught off guard on Wednesday as President-elect Trump demanded that a debt ceiling hike be paired with a stopgap government funding bill, rejecting a sweeping bipartisan plan to avert a government shutdown this week.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Fani Willis disqualified as prosecutor on Trump Georgia election case
item.description: A Georgia appeals court booted Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis's (D) office from the 2020 election interference case against President-elect Trump on Thursday due to her relationship with a top prosecutor on the case.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Mike Johnson will 'easily remain speaker' if he acts 'decisively and tough' on spending bill
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: President-elect Donald Trump told Fox News Digital that House Speaker Mike Johnson will “easily remain speaker" for the next Congress if he “acts decisively and tough" and eliminates “all of the traps being set by Democrats" in the spending package.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Party switch paying off? Valdes secures committee leadership after joining GOP
item.description: One state Representative in the Tampa Bay area made the controversial move to switch political parties, just last week. The news came was greeted with both cheers and criticisms.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republicans from hurricane-ravaged states warn against stripping disaster aid from spending bill
item.description: Senate Republicans from regions affected by hurricanes and other storms are warning negotiators against stripping disaster funding from the stopgap spending bill that is suddenly in turmoil after President-elect Trump heavily criticized Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) plan.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rand Paul blocks bill to authorize local, state authorities to track drones
item.description: Paul argued the bill would have expanded the government’s surveillance powers and downplayed concerns that the drones pose a threat to the public.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Congress unveils funding deal with more than $100 billion in disaster aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Congressional leaders have unveiled a stopgap spending bill that will keep the federal government funded through March 14 and provide more than $100 billion in emergency aid to help states and local communities recover from Hurricanes Helene and Milton and other natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Congress strikes deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional leaders have struck a bipartisan deal to keep the government funded beyond a looming Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump sues Des Moines Register, pollster
item.description: “Selzer’s polling ‘miss’ was not an astonishing coincidence — it was intentional,” the complaint states.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: New York judge says Trump is not immune from hush money conviction
item.description: A spokesperson for President-elect Trump said the case should never have been brought, called for its immediate dismissal.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Trump weighs in on NYC mayor, vaccines and drones in freewheeling press conference at Mar-a-Lago
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump told reporters that he would consider pardoning embattled New York Mayor Eric Adams, said the country was “not going to lose” the polio vaccine and weighed in on the flurry of drone sightings over New Jersey during a freewheeling press conference Monday at his Mar-a-Lago
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
